title: executing-plans

goal.text: Execute an existing written implementation plan with critical review gates, blocker-aware stopping behavior, and explicit finish-branch handoff.

requirements.summary: Use this only when a written implementation plan already exists and should be executed in a separate session with review checkpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: The workflow checks whether subagent-driven-development should be used instead.
The plan is reviewed critically before implementation begins.
Execution follows the written plan step by step and runs the specified verification.
Completion hands off to finishing-a-development-branch instead of free-form closeout.

tasks.title: Assess fit and choose the execution path
tasks.detail:
Confirm that a written implementation plan already exists, then decide whether to execute it here or switch to `subagent-driven-development` because subagents are available and the work can be decomposed safely.

Use [CLAW-KNOWLEDGE.md](/D:/Users/chany/Documents/claw-kit/packages/codex-adapter/skills/executing-plans/CLAW-KNOWLEDGE.md) for the routing rules and guardrails.

continue-here.summary: Stay in executing-plans and move into the review gate.
nextsteps: Keep the written plan and compiled knowledge open.

switch-to-subagent-driven-development.summary: Do not execute the plan here; route the work into the stronger subagent-driven workflow.
nextsteps: Tell the partner that subagent support makes this workflow stronger.
Use the subagent-driven-development skill instead of continuing here.

tasks.title: Load the plan and clear the review gate
tasks.detail:
Read the written plan, review it critically, and decide whether it is safe to start execution. If concerns, blockers, or critical gaps are found, raise them before making changes.

This preserves the source skill's hard rule that review happens before implementation.

ready-to-execute.summary: The plan passed review and execution can begin.
nextsteps: Advance tasks exactly as written and run the specified verification.

raise-concerns.summary: Execution is paused until the partner resolves the plan concerns.
nextsteps: Do not guess through the gaps.
Return to review after the plan or assumptions are updated.

tasks.title: Execute each planned task with verification checkpoints
tasks.detail: For each planned task, mark it in progress, follow the written steps exactly, run the specified verification, and mark it complete. Stop immediately if a blocker from the source stop list appears.

default.summary: Planned execution is complete and ready for the required finish workflow.
nextsteps: Check the blocker list one more time.
Move into the required finishing-a-development-branch handoff.

tasks.title: Redirect or complete through the required finish workflow
tasks.detail: If task 1 chose the subagent path, stop here and switch to `subagent-driven-development`. If task 2 found review concerns, stop here and raise them. If task 3 finished execution, announce the handoff to `finishing-a-development-branch` and use that skill to verify tests, present completion options, and execute the chosen finish path.

references.why: Preserves the control flow, stop conditions, and downstream skill handoffs from the source skill.
Keeps the full original superpowers wording, including the explicit subagent recommendation and stop rules.
Maps source information to the converted homes for verification and maintenance.

rules: Do not use this skill unless a written implementation plan already exists.
If subagents are available and the work can be decomposed safely, switch to subagent-driven-development instead of executing here.
Review the plan critically before implementation; do not skip straight to code changes.
Follow the written plan exactly and run the specified verification for each task.
Stop and ask for clarification instead of guessing through blockers or repeated verification failures.
Do not start implementation on main or master without explicit user consent.
After execution, hand off to finishing-a-development-branch instead of improvising the closeout.
